title: Essere e stare: due verbi per "to be"
grammar_features_introduced: present tense of essere: sono, sei, è, siamo, siete, sono
the sono 1sg/3pl syncretism and how context disambiguates
present tense of stare: sto, stai, sta, stiamo, state, stanno
irregularity of stare (and parallel fare, dare)
semantic split between essere (identity, origin, traits, feelings, time, neutral location) and stare (health, progressive, stare per, fixed expressions, colloquial location)
preview of essere as passato-prossimo auxiliary
pronunciation_features_introduced: è with grave accent vs e without (distinct words, distinct sounds)
notes: Third A1 grammar lesson. Motivated by the sono syncretism question that arose while reading the cooking chapter — this lesson makes the 1sg/3pl collision explicit, then pivots to the broader essere/stare split. Spanish speakers get a dedicated comparison section since their ser/estar instincts don’t line up exactly with Italian. Ends with a forward reference to the avere auxiliary lesson.
